623	  in \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\). This is an \emph{objectwise}
624	  existence-of-isomorphism statement for the three fixed invertible objects
625	  \(M, N, P\) --- not a natural associator on a category --- and that is
626	  exactly the datum consumed by the group law on \(\otimes\)-iso-classes
627	  (\cref{lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup}), where associativity is the
628	  proposition \(\mathrm{Nonempty}\bigl((M \otimes_X N) \otimes_X P \cong
629	  M \otimes_X (N \otimes_X P)\bigr)\).
630	\end{lemma}
631	
632	\begin{proof}
633	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj, def:scheme_modules_isinvertible,
634	        lem:flat_whisker_localizer}
635	  The associator is built directly from the presheaf-of-modules monoidal data
636	  by absorbing the two nested sheafifications, with the only non-formal input
637	  being the flat-whiskering bridge \cref{lem:flat_whisker_localizer}.
638	
639	  \emph{Notation.} Write \(a = \mathtt{PresheafOfModules.sheafification}\) for the
640	  sheafification functor and \(M^{\flat}\) for the underlying presheaf of an
641	  object \(M\), so that the substrate of \cref{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj} is
642	  \(M \otimes_X N = a\bigl(M^{\flat} \otimes^{p} N^{\flat}\bigr)\), with
643	  \(\otimes^{p}\) the presheaf-of-modules tensor product
644	  (\texttt{Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Monoidal.PresheafOfModules}). Let
645	  \(\eta = \mathtt{toSheafify}\) be the sheafification unit, the canonical
646	  morphism \(A \to a(A)^{\flat}\); it lies in the sheafification localizer class
647	  \(J.W\), being locally injective and locally surjective
648	  (\(\mathtt{instIsLocallyInjectiveToSheafify}\)). Let \(\alpha\) be the
649	  associator of the presheaf-of-modules monoidal category,
650	  \[
651	    \alpha \;\colon\;
652	      (M^{\flat} \otimes^{p} N^{\flat}) \otimes^{p} P^{\flat}
653	        \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;
654	      M^{\flat} \otimes^{p} (N^{\flat} \otimes^{p} P^{\flat}).
655	  \]
656	
657	  \emph{Flatness is free.} Since \(M, N, P\) are \(\otimes\)-invertible they are
658	  projective, hence flat (Mathlib
659	  \(\mathtt{Module.Invertible} \Rightarrow \mathtt{Projective} \Rightarrow
660	  \mathtt{Flat}\), via \(\mathtt{Module.Flat.of\_projective}\)). The flatness
661	  hypothesis of \cref{lem:flat_whisker_localizer} is therefore satisfied by each
662	  of \(M, N, P\) at no cost.
663	
664	  \emph{The nested form.} Unfolding the substrate on both sides exposes an inner
665	  sheafification:
666	  \[
667	    (M \otimes_X N) \otimes_X P
668	      \;=\; a\Bigl(\, a\bigl(M^{\flat} \otimes^{p} N^{\flat}\bigr)^{\flat}
669	              \otimes^{p} P^{\flat} \,\Bigr),
670	    \qquad
671	    M \otimes_X (N \otimes_X P)
672	      \;=\; a\Bigl(\, M^{\flat} \otimes^{p}
673	              a\bigl(N^{\flat} \otimes^{p} P^{\flat}\bigr)^{\flat} \,\Bigr).
674	  \]
675	  The required isomorphism is the three-step composite:
676	  \begin{enumerate}
677	    \item \emph{Absorb the inner sheafification on the left.} The unit
678	      \(\eta_{M^{\flat} \otimes^{p} N^{\flat}}\) lies in \(J.W\); its
679	      right-whiskering by the flat object \(P\),
680	      \(\eta_{M^{\flat} \otimes^{p} N^{\flat}} \triangleright P^{\flat}\),
681	      again lies in \(J.W\) by \cref{lem:flat_whisker_localizer} (\(P\) flat).
682	      Applying the sheafification functor \(a\) inverts it:
683	      \[
684	        a\Bigl(\, a\bigl(M^{\flat} \otimes^{p} N^{\flat}\bigr)^{\flat}
685	              \otimes^{p} P^{\flat} \,\Bigr)
686	          \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;
687	        a\bigl(\, (M^{\flat} \otimes^{p} N^{\flat}) \otimes^{p} P^{\flat} \,\bigr).
688	      \]
689	    \item \emph{Transport the presheaf associator.} Apply \(a\) to \(\alpha\):
690	      \[
691	        a\bigl(\, (M^{\flat} \otimes^{p} N^{\flat}) \otimes^{p} P^{\flat} \,\bigr)
692	          \;\xrightarrow[\ \sim\ ]{\;a.\mathtt{mapIso}\,\alpha\;}\;
693	        a\bigl(\, M^{\flat} \otimes^{p} (N^{\flat} \otimes^{p} P^{\flat}) \,\bigr).
694	      \]
695	    \item \emph{Restore the inner sheafification on the right.} The unit
696	      \(\eta_{N^{\flat} \otimes^{p} P^{\flat}}\) lies in \(J.W\); its
697	      left-whiskering by the flat object \(M\),
698	      \(M^{\flat} \triangleleft \eta_{N^{\flat} \otimes^{p} P^{\flat}}\), again
699	      lies in \(J.W\) by \cref{lem:flat_whisker_localizer} (\(M\) flat). Applying
700	      \(a\) inverts it:
701	      \[
702	        a\bigl(\, M^{\flat} \otimes^{p} (N^{\flat} \otimes^{p} P^{\flat}) \,\bigr)
703	          \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;
704	        a\Bigl(\, M^{\flat} \otimes^{p}
705	              a\bigl(N^{\flat} \otimes^{p} P^{\flat}\bigr)^{\flat} \,\Bigr)
706	          \;=\; M \otimes_X (N \otimes_X P).
707	      \]
708	  \end{enumerate}
709	  Composing the three isomorphisms yields
710	  \((M \otimes_X N) \otimes_X P \xrightarrow{\sim} M \otimes_X (N \otimes_X P)\).
711	  Only steps 1 and 3 carry non-formal content, and that content is exactly the
712	  flat-whiskering bridge \cref{lem:flat_whisker_localizer}; step 2 is the image
713	  under \(a\) of the presheaf monoidal associator already supplied by Mathlib.
714	
715	  This is an objectwise isomorphism for the three fixed invertible objects, not
716	  a natural associator on a category: no pentagon identity is constructed or
717	  used. The group law on \(\otimes\)-iso-classes
718	  (\cref{lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup}) consumes only the existence of this
719	  isomorphism, \(\mathrm{Nonempty}(\,\cdots \cong \cdots)\), so this is all that
720	  is required. The construction uses
721	  \emph{no} \(\mathtt{MonoidalClosed}(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\,R)\),
722	  \emph{no} open-immersion restriction-compatibility isomorphism
723	  (\cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}), and \emph{no} per-object local
724	  trivialisation or gluing cocycle on an affine cover: the two absorption
725	  isomorphisms are obtained directly from flatness of the whiskering object via
726	  \cref{lem:flat_whisker_localizer}, without instantiating any bundled monoidal
727	  structure on the localizer class.
728	\end{proof}

38	
39	### Decision 1: which realization of the invertible-scoped associator
40	
41	The associator nests an inner sheafification inside the presheaf tensor on both sides:
42	`(M⊗N)⊗P = a( a(M.val ⊗ᵖ N.val).val ⊗ᵖ P.val )` where `a = PresheafOfModules.sheafification`.
43	Transporting the presheaf associator `α` needs, besides `a.mapIso(α)`, the
44	**absorption iso** `a(a(A).val ⊗ᵖ B) ≅ a(A ⊗ᵖ B)` (and its mirror) = whiskering-stability
45	of the sheafification localizer `J.W`: `J.W g → J.W (B ◁ g)`.
46	
47	- **Mathlib idiom**. Mathlib's general whiskering-stability of a sheafification localizer
48	  is `CategoryTheory.GrothendieckTopology.W.whiskerLeft`
49	  (`Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Sites.Monoidal`), whose signature **requires
50	  `[MonoidalClosed A]`** plus enriched-hom instances on `Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A`. For invertibility/Picard,
51	  Mathlib instead scopes to a *predicate on objects* — `Module.Invertible R M`
52	  (`Mathlib.RingTheory.PicardGroup`) — and derives `Module.Invertible.instFinite`,
53	  `Module.Invertible.instProjective`, hence `Module.Flat.of_projective` (all present), so the
54	  invertible/line-bundle objects are FLAT, and flat-exactness replaces the closed structure.
55	- **Realization (1) — local-trivialization gluing**. To build `(M⊗N)⊗P ≅ M⊗(N⊗P)` by gluing
56	  structure-sheaf associators on a trivialising cover, you must first identify
57	  `((M⊗N)⊗P).restrict W` with the affine triple tensor — i.e. invoke
58	  `tensorObj_restrict_iso`. That lemma is a `sorry`; its residual (H1/H2, file L357–398) is
59	  the strong-monoidal-pushforward / `restrictScalars`-along-ring-iso infrastructure,
60	  **absent from Mathlib and flatness-INDEPENDENT** (H2 is about the open-immersion ring iso
61	  `f.appIso`, not about M,N,P). So scoping M,N,P to invertible does **not** lighten it.
62	  The cited precedent `tensorObj_isLocallyTrivial` proves only EXISTENCE of local
63	  trivialisations yet still routes through the same `sorry` — `lean_verify` confirms
64	  `sorryAx` in its axioms. **Gap: divergent-and-wrong** (renamed wall).
65	- **Realization (3) — `J.W.IsMonoidal` on the flat subcategory**. Gating Mathlib's
66	  `CategoryTheory.Sheaf.monoidalCategory` needs a `MorphismProperty.IsMonoidal J.W` instance,
67	  whose `whiskerLeft` field is exactly `GrothendieckTopology.W.whiskerLeft` — **`MonoidalClosed`-
68	  gated**. Even restricted to flat objects, instantiating Mathlib's bundled `W.IsMonoidal`
69	  pulls in the closed structure for ALL objects. **Gap: divergent-with-cost** (the wall, packaged).
70	- **Realization (2) — flat-exactness whiskerLeft (RECOMMENDED)**. Do NOT instantiate the
71	  bundled `W.IsMonoidal`. Prove the two specific absorption-iso *instances* directly:
72	  `a(η_{M⊗N} ◁ P)` is iso (P invertible⇒flat) and the mirror `a(M ▷ η_{N⊗P})` is iso
73	  (M invertible⇒flat), where `η = toSheafify` is the sheafification unit (in `J.W` by
74	  `instIsLocallyInjectiveToSheafify`). The bridge `J.W g → J.W (P ◁ g)` for FLAT P is
75	  elementary: via the `J.WEqualsLocallyBijective` characterisation (`Presheaf.IsLocallyInjective`
76	  + `IsLocallySurjective`), `P ◁ g` stays locally injective by
77	  `Module.Flat.lTensor_preserves_injective_linearMap` (sectionwise) and locally surjective by
78	  right-exactness of `⊗`. **All named ingredients present in Mathlib.** Assemble:
79	  `(M⊗N)⊗P ≅[a(η◁P)] a((M⊗ᵖN)⊗ᵖP) ≅[a.mapIso α] a(M⊗ᵖ(N⊗ᵖP)) ≅[a(M▷η)] M⊗(N⊗P)`.
80	  **Gap: divergent-equivalent → ALIGN_WITH_MATHLIB** (this is the `Module.Invertible`+flat idiom).
81	- **Verdict**: realization (2) — **ALIGN_WITH_MATHLIB**; realizations (1) and (3) — reject
82	  (both bottom out in absent Mathlib: (1) in `tensorObj_restrict_iso`'s H1/H2, (3) in
83	  `MonoidalClosed`).
84	
85	### Decision 2: re-scope `lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso` from arbitrary `M,N,P` to invertible/flat
86	
87	- **Mathlib idiom**. `Module.Invertible` is a `Prop` object-predicate; the Picard arithmetic
88	  (`CommRing.Pic.mk_tensor`, `mk_dual`, …) are existence-of-iso facts scoped to invertibles,
89	  never coherence on all modules. The flatness the bridge "needs" is FREE on invertibles
90	  (`Module.Invertible.instProjective` → `Module.Flat.of_projective`).
91	- **Project's path**. Blueprint L536 states the associator for *arbitrary* `M,N,P` with "no
92	  line-bundle, local-freeness or flatness hypothesis required" — which is precisely what forces
93	  the arbitrary-`F` whiskering-stability = `MonoidalClosed` wall (its own `% NOTE` L549–562).
94	- **Gap**: divergent-with-cost. The arbitrary statement is unbuildable at the pinned commit; the
95	  consumer `lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup`/`thm:rel_pic_addcommgroup_via_tensorobj` only ever
96	  associates INVERTIBLE objects.
97	- **Verdict**: **ALIGN_WITH_MATHLIB** — re-scope the hypothesis to `IsInvertible M/N/P` (or a
98	  `Module.Flat`-style predicate); drop the "no flatness required" clause.
99	
100	## Recommendation
101	
102	Re-scope `lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso` to invertible `M,N,P` and formalize it via **realization (2)**,
103	the flat-exactness whiskerLeft. The associator is built as a 3-step composite —
104	`a(η ◁ P)` absorption (P flat), `a.mapIso` of the presheaf associator `α`, mirror `a(M ▷ η)`
105	absorption (M flat) — whose only non-`mapIso` content is the bridge lemma
106	`J.W g → J.W (P ◁ g)` for flat `P`, assembled from `Module.Flat.lTensor_preserves_injective_linearMap`
107	+ right-exactness through the `J.WEqualsLocallyBijective` / `IsLocallyInjective`+`IsLocallySurjective`
108	API (all present Mathlib). This genuinely avoids `MonoidalClosed (PresheafOfModules R₀)`.

111	residual (H1 presheaf pushforward-adjunction, H2 strong-monoidal `restrictScalars`-along-ring-iso)

113	"already proven" precedent `tensorObj_isLocallyTrivial` is itself `sorryAx`-tainted via the same
114	hole. Do **NOT** take realization (3): instantiating Mathlib's bundled `W.IsMonoidal` is gated on
115	`GrothendieckTopology.W.whiskerLeft`'s `[MonoidalClosed A]` requirement — the wall, renamed.
116	
117	**Reversal signal: NOT triggered globally.** Realization (2) is a genuine present-Mathlib escape,

1174	  Defs.~df:aPf + df:Pfs (the target category is the category of abelian
1175	  groups; the relative Picard functor is a quotient of abelian groups).}
1176	  Let \(C/k\) be a smooth proper geometrically integral curve over a
1177	  field \(k\), let \(\pi_C : C \to \Spec k\) be the structure morphism,
1178	  and let \(T\) be a \(k\)-scheme with structure morphism
1179	  \(\pi_T : T \to \Spec k\). The set
1180	  \[
1181	    \Pic^{\sharp}_{C/k}(T) \;\;:=\;\; \Pic(C \times_k T) \,/\, \pi_T^*\Pic(T)
1182	  \]
1183	  of \cref{thm:relative_pic_quotient_well_defined} carries a canonical
1184	  abelian-group structure with addition
1185	  \(\bigl[\,\mathcal{L}\,\bigr] + \bigl[\,\mathcal{L}'\,\bigr]
1186	   := \bigl[\,\mathcal{L} \otimes_{C \times_k T} \mathcal{L}'\,\bigr]\),
1187	  neutral element \(\bigl[\,\mathcal{O}_{C \times_k T}\,\bigr]\), and
1188	  inverse
1189	  \(-\bigl[\,\mathcal{L}\,\bigr] := \bigl[\,\mathcal{L}^{-1}\,\bigr]\),
1190	  where \(\otimes_{C \times_k T}\) and \((-)^{-1}\) are the operations
1191	  supplied by the substrate of \cref{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
1192	  restricted to the \(\texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct}\,\pi_C\,\pi_T\) carrier
1193	  via \cref{lem:tensorobj_lift_onproduct}. With this structure the quotient
1194	  map
1195	  \(\Pic(C \times_k T) \twoheadrightarrow \Pic^{\sharp}_{C/k}(T)\) is a
1196	  surjective group homomorphism with kernel exactly \(\pi_T^*\Pic(T)\),
1197	  in agreement with \cref{lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid}.
1198	\end{theorem}

1200	\begin{proof}
1201	  \uses{lem:tensorobj_lift_onproduct,
1202	        lem:pullback_compatible_with_tensorobj,
1203	        def:scheme_modules_isinvertible,
1204	        lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup,
1205	        thm:relative_pic_quotient_well_defined,
1206	        lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid}
1207	  Write \(\Pic(C \times_k T)\) for the set of isomorphism classes of objects
1208	  of \(\texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct}\,\pi_C\,\pi_T\) (line bundles on
1209	  \(C \times_k T\)), with the operation
1210	  \([L] + [L'] := [L \otimes_{C \times_k T} L']\). This operation is
1211	  well-defined on isomorphism classes: an isomorphism \(L \cong L_0\) tensors
1212	  to an isomorphism \(L \otimes L' \cong L_0 \otimes L'\) by functoriality of
1213	  \(\otimes\) (\cref{lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality}), and
1214	  symmetrically in the second argument. The abelian-group structure on
1215	  \(\Pic(C \times_k T)\) is then exactly the \emph{units group of the
1216	  commutative monoid of \(\otimes\)-iso-classes}
1217	  (\cref{lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup}): multiplication
1218	  \([L]\cdot[L'] = [L \otimes_{C\times_k T} L']\) is well-defined and
1219	  commutative-monoidal by the coherence isomorphisms
1220	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso,lem:tensorobj_unit_iso,lem:tensorobj_comm_iso},
1221	  and an iso-class is a unit precisely when its carrier is
1222	  \(\otimes\)-invertible (\cref{def:scheme_modules_isinvertible}), the inverse
1223	  being carried by the predicate. Each axiom is a \emph{proposition} --- the
1224	  existence of an isomorphism --- so no monoidal-coherence datum (pentagon,
1225	  triangle, hexagon) is invoked; the \(\AddCommGroup\) on
1226	  \(\Pic(C \times_k T)\) is the additive form of this units group. This mirrors
1227	  Mathlib's own Picard group: \(\mathtt{CommRing.Pic}\,R\) carries its
1228	  \(\mathtt{CommGroup}\) (\(\mathtt{instCommGroupPic}\),
1229	  \texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.PicardGroup}) transported from
1230	  \(\mathrm{Units}\bigl(\mathrm{Skeleton}(\mathtt{ModuleCat}\,R)\bigr)\); here
1231	  the same \(\mathrm{Units}(\cdots)\) shape is transported to the scheme-level
1232	  \(\otimes_X\). (The scheme-level Picard group is genuinely absent from
1233	  Mathlib --- \texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.PicardGroup} records ``connect to
1234	  invertible sheaves'' as an open task --- so the project supplies it lightly.)
1235	  The invertibility-predicate carrier is the project's \(\mathtt{IsInvertible}\)
1236	  (\cref{def:scheme_modules_isinvertible}), the scheme-level analogue of
1237	  Mathlib's \(\mathtt{Module.Invertible}\); the geometric predicate
1238	  \(\mathtt{IsLocallyTrivial}\) is retained separately and is connected to
1239	  \(\mathtt{IsInvertible}\) only by an off-critical-path equivalence (only the
1240	  geometric \(\mathtt{IsInvertible} \Leftarrow \mathtt{IsLocallyTrivial}\)-style
1241	  direction is nontrivial, and it is not needed here).
1242	
1243	  By \cref{lem:pullback_compatible_with_tensorobj}, the pullback functor
1244	  \(\pi_T^{*} : \texttt{LineBundle}\,T \to
1245	   \texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct}\,\pi_C\,\pi_T\) carries tensor products to
1246	  tensor products and the unit to the unit, so the induced map on isomorphism
1247	  classes \(\pi_T^{*} : \Pic(T) \to \Pic(C \times_k T)\) is a group
1248	  homomorphism, and its image is a subgroup.
1249	
1250	  By \cref{thm:relative_pic_quotient_well_defined}, the underlying set of
1251	  \(\Pic^{\sharp}_{C/k}(T)\) is the set of cosets of this subgroup. The
1252	  quotient of an abelian group by a subgroup is itself an abelian group, via
1253	  the standard \(\mathtt{QuotientAddGroup}\) construction, and the quotient
1254	  map is a surjective group homomorphism whose kernel is exactly the subgroup
1255	  (Stacks tag 01CR for the Picard-group special case; the general
1256	  quotient-of-abelian-groups statement is in any algebra textbook). Putting
1257	  the pieces together gives the \(\AddCommGroup\) instance on
1258	  \(\Pic^{\sharp}_{C/k}(T)\) of the statement, and identifies it with the
1259	  structure of \cref{lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid}.
1260	\end{proof}

/-- **Flat right-whiskering preserves the sheafification localizer.** The
braiding-conjugate of `W_whiskerLeft_of_flat`: for a sectionwise-flat presheaf of
modules `F` and a morphism `g` in the sheafification localizer `J.W`, the
right-whiskered morphism `g ▷ F` again lies in `J.W`. Obtained from the
left-whiskered statement by conjugating with the (iso) braiding of the symmetric
presheaf-of-modules monoidal structure, using that `J.W` respects isomorphisms. -/
lemma W_whiskerRight_of_flat [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective Ab.{u}]
    (F : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))
    [∀ X, Module.Flat (R.obj X) (F.obj X)]
    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N)
    (hg : J.W ((toPresheaf _).map g)) :
    J.W ((toPresheaf _).map (g ▷ F)) := by
  have hwl := W_whiskerLeft_of_flat F g hg
  -- `g ▷ F = (β_ M F).hom ≫ (F ◁ g) ≫ (β_ N F).inv` by braiding naturality.
  have hconj : g ▷ F
      = (BraidedCategory.braiding M F).hom ≫ (F ◁ g) ≫ (BraidedCategory.braiding N F).inv := by
    rw [← BraidedCategory.braiding_naturality_left g F]
    simp
  rw [hconj, Functor.map_comp, Functor.map_comp]
  -- `J.W` respects isos on both sides (it is the sheafification localizer).
  rw [(J.W).cancel_left_of_respectsIso, (J.W).cancel_right_of_respectsIso]
  exact hwl

/-- **The sheafification-localization bridge.** A morphism `f` of presheaves of
modules whose underlying additive-presheaf map lies in the sheafification localizer
`J.W` is sent by the associated-sheaf-of-modules functor to an isomorphism. This is
the single residual of the `⊗`-invertibility associator
`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_assoc_iso`. It is the morphism-property
identity `PresheafOfModules.inverseImage_W_toPresheaf_eq_inverseImage_isomorphisms`
(the sheafification functor *is* the localization at `J.W.inverseImage (toPresheaf _)`)
read at a single morphism. -/
lemma isIso_sheafification_map_of_W
    {R₀ : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat} {Rsh : CategoryTheory.Sheaf J RingCat} (α : R₀ ⟶ Rsh.obj)
    [Presheaf.IsLocallyInjective J α] [Presheaf.IsLocallySurjective J α]
    [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective AddCommGrpCat] [CategoryTheory.HasWeakSheafify J AddCommGrpCat]
    {A B : PresheafOfModules.{u} R₀} (f : A ⟶ B)
    (hf : J.W ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf R₀).map f)) :
    IsIso ((PresheafOfModules.sheafification α).map f) := by
  have h := PresheafOfModules.inverseImage_W_toPresheaf_eq_inverseImage_isomorphisms (J := J) α
  have h2 : (CategoryTheory.MorphismProperty.isomorphisms (SheafOfModules Rsh)).inverseImage
      (PresheafOfModules.sheafification α) f := by rw [← h]; exact hf
  exact h2

end FlatWhisker

**Designated go/no-go bridge CLEARED axiom-clean. The associator did NOT close —
a NEW, previously-unrecognized blocker was located: the route requires

derivable from `IsInvertible` (and is false in general). The blueprint's
associator proof has a real gap.**

- **+ `PresheafOfModules.W_whiskerRight_of_flat`** — braiding-conjugate of the
  existing `W_whiskerLeft_of_flat`. **CLOSED, axiom-clean.**
- `tensorObj_assoc_iso` — still a typed `sorry`; docstring rewritten with the
  corrected residual analysis (was: "single residual = the bridge"; now: "bridge
  closed; the genuine residual is the sectionwise-flatness feeder").
- Off-path sorries unchanged: `tensorObj_restrict_iso`, `exists_tensorObj_inverse`,
  `addCommGroup_via_tensorObj`.

## isIso_sheafification_map_of_W (the bridge) — RESOLVED

### Attempt 1 — RESOLVED, axiom-clean
- **Approach:** The plan's recipe (toPresheaf reflects isos + AddCommGrp
  sheafification is localization + compatibility iso) is more work than needed.
  Mathlib already ships the exact identity:
  `PresheafOfModules.inverseImage_W_toPresheaf_eq_inverseImage_isomorphisms` :
  `J.W.inverseImage (toPresheaf R₀) = (isomorphisms (SheafOfModules R)).inverseImage (sheafification α)`
  (module `Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Sheaf.Localization`; companion
  `instIsLocalization…SheafificationInverseImage…` says `sheafification α` IS the
  localization at `J.W.inverseImage (toPresheaf R₀)`).
- **Result:** RESOLVED. Read at one morphism `f`: `J.W (toPresheaf.map f)` is
  `(J.W.inverseImage (toPresheaf R₀)) f`; rewrite by the identity gives
  `(isomorphisms _).inverseImage (sheafification α) f` = `IsIso (sheafification.map f)`.
  3-line proof. **The localization characterisation the plan flagged `[expected]`
  is genuinely present in Mathlib.**

## W_whiskerRight_of_flat — RESOLVED

### Attempt 1 — RESOLVED, axiom-clean
- **Approach:** `g ▷ F = (β_ M F).hom ≫ (F ◁ g) ≫ (β_ N F).inv` via
  `BraidedCategory.braiding_naturality_left` (reassociate first:
  `rw [← Category.assoc, ← braiding_naturality_left g F, Category.assoc, Iso.hom_inv_id, Category.comp_id]`),
  then `Functor.map_comp` and `J.W.cancel_left/right_of_respectsIso` to strip the
  iso braidings, landing on `W_whiskerLeft_of_flat F g hg`.
- **Result:** RESOLVED.

## tensorObj_assoc_iso (line ~568) — PARTIAL (structure clear, blocked on flatness)

### Attempt 1 — three-step composite via bridge — BLOCKED on sectionwise flatness
- **Approach:** Blueprint's 3-step composite. `a = sheafification (𝟙 ringCatSheaf.val)`,
  `η = sheafificationAdjunction.unit` (toPresheaf-image `toSheafify ∈ J.W` via
  `GrothendieckTopology.W_toSheafify` + `toPresheaf_map_sheafificationAdjunction_unit_app`).
  Step1 `a(η_{M⊗N} ▷ P.val)` iso, step2 `a.mapIso α`, step3 `a(M.val ◁ η_{N⊗P})` iso.
  Each absorption iso = `isIso_sheafification_map_of_W (W_whiskerRight/Left_of_flat … (W_toSheafify))`.
- **Result:** PARTIAL — the `IsIso` step-1 reasoning typechecks structurally, but
  two hard obstacles block full closure:

- **BLOCKER 1 (mathematical — the real one): sectionwise flatness is not
  derivable from `IsInvertible`.** `W_whiskerLeft/Right_of_flat` need the instance
  `∀ U : (Opens X)ᵒᵖ, Module.Flat (𝒪_X(U)) (P.val.obj U)` (resp. M). The ONLY
  Mathlib path to `Module.Flat` here is single-ring
  `Module.Invertible R m → Module.Projective → Module.Flat.of_projective`
  (confirmed via `lean_leansearch`/`lean_loogle`: no "invertible sheaf ⇒ flat
  sections" lemma exists). That path needs `P.val.obj U` to be an invertible
  `𝒪_X(U)`-module for **every** open `U` — FALSE in general. The site of
  `Scheme.Modules X` is `TopCat.Sheaf` over **all** of `Opens X` (verified:
  `Scheme.ringCatSheaf : TopCat.Sheaf RingCat X.toPresheafedSpace`), not just
  affines. Over a non-affine open the global-sections functor is not exact and
  does not preserve flatness/invertibility; invertibility is a local/affine
  property. **The blueprint proof of `lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso` ("Since M,N,P are
  ⊗-invertible they are projective, hence flat") conflates global module-
  invertibility with sectionwise-over-all-opens flatness.** This is a genuine gap
  in the blueprint, not a Lean technicality.

  - **Correct fix (mathematically sound):** local injectivity of `η ▷ P` is a
    LOCAL property; on the cover where `P ≅ 𝒪` it reduces to `η` itself (locally
    injective). So the right lemma is `isLocallyInjective_whiskerRight_of_locallyTrivial`
    (use local triviality, not global sectionwise flatness). This needs
    `IsInvertible ⇒ IsLocallyTrivial` (blueprint-flagged nontrivial) and a
    sieve-refinement rerun of the flat injectivity argument — substantial new
    infrastructure (~150–250 LOC across several sub-lemmas), NOT a single
    prove-mode close.

- **BLOCKER 2 (Lean friction, compounding):** `X.ringCatSheaf.val` is only DEFEQ
  to `X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat`, not syntactically equal. The
  adjunction unit `η`'s type is phrased over `Sheaf.val X.ringCatSheaf`, so
  (a) the monoidal `▷` notation fails `MonoidalCategoryStruct` synthesis (worked
  around with explicit `whiskerRight (C := PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ …))`),
  and (b) the `Module.Flat` instance is keyed on `X.presheaf.obj Y` but
  `W_whisker*_of_flat` synthesizes the ring as `(sheafToPresheaf …).obj X.sheaf`
  → `synthInstance` heartbeat timeout (20000). The whole def also hits
  `whnf` `maxHeartbeats` (200000) timeouts on the nested-sheafification defeqs.
  Even granting BLOCKER 1, this friction makes the composite extremely fragile.

- **Dead end:** do NOT pursue `∀ U, Module.Flat (𝒪_X(U)) (P.val U)` from
  `IsInvertible` — not provable (likely false). Do NOT use
  `W_whiskerLeft/Right_of_flat` with `F = M.val/P.val` directly.
- **Next step / recommendation for plan agent:** decide between
  (i) **rescope** `lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso` + `IsInvertible` so a flatness/locally-
  free hypothesis is available (but the CommMonoid consumer only has
  `IsInvertible`, so this needs `IsInvertible ⇒ IsLocallyTrivial ⇒ local-free`
  first); (ii) **build the local-triviality whiskering lemma** as a multi-iter
  sub-build; or (iii) **escalate**. NB: the designated go/no-go bridge CLEARED,
  so this is NOT the pre-committed reversal trigger as worded (that fired on the
  bridge bottoming out, which did not happen) — but the associator's stated proof
  is nonetheless blocked by a newly-found blueprint gap.

## Blueprint marker guidance (for review agent)
- `lem:flat_whisker_localizer` (`W_whiskerLeft_of_flat`) — already `\leanok`; the
  new conjugate `W_whiskerRight_of_flat` is NOT blueprint-pinned (descriptive).
- The new bridge `isIso_sheafification_map_of_W` is NOT blueprint-pinned; consider

- `lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso` (`tensorObj_assoc_iso`) — still `sorry`; sync_leanok

  mathematically incorrect as written** (the "invertible ⇒ flat" step is

- `lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup` (`tensorObjIsoclassCommMonoid`) — NOT
  attempted this iter: it `\uses` the associator, which did not close. Building it
  would inherit the associator's sorry.

## Negative search results
- Searched "invertible module is flat" / loogle `Module.Invertible, Module.Flat`:
  only single-ring `Module.Flat.of_projective` + the PicardGroup-internal
  `instInvertibleSubtypeMem…`. No sheaf-sections flatness.
- No `BraidedCategory.braiding_naturality_left` via loogle by name, but it exists
